methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $231M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $529M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 25.3x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: SambaNova Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series D), post-money $5B

private.name: Tenstorrent
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series D), post-money $2.7B

data.narrative:
Pragmatic Semiconductor is a Series D chip design company that has raised $231M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 8 private and 3 public semiconductor firms, including peers SambaNova Systems, Tenstorrent, and DEEPX at the Series C/D stage.

Two valuation methods prove applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.5x step-up multiplier typical for Series D advancement to the $231M capital raised proxy, yielding $346M. Peer-median analysis anchors to three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables with a median valuation of $2.7B and a range of $529M to $5.0B. Public-comp anchoring using 25.3x median price-to-sales across ALAB, NVDA, and AMD cannot be applied without Pragmatic's revenue, which remains undisclosed.

The triangulated analysis across applicable methods produces a valuation range of $295M (low) to $3.1B (high), with a median of $1.5B. The wide dispersion reflects both the inherent stage-specific uncertainty in early-stage semiconductor valuations and material divergence among peer multiples—SambaNova at $5.0B versus DEEPX at $529M. Key uncertainties include Pragmatic's proprietary technology differentiation relative to peers, revenue trajectory, and capital efficiency. Revenue disclosure would enable public-comp anchoring and tighten the valuation range materially.
